title: Research Index
blurb: swift-ownership-primitives ships a package-local corpus that groups (1) the cross-type merit + completeness + naming review (v2.1.0) and (2) per-module naming research for every type whose name was DEBATE / QUESTION / INCORRECT in v2.1.0 — grounded in Apple (Swift Evolution, stdlib, API Design Guidelines) and academia (linear/affine types, ownership types, session types). Ecosystem-wide cross-references live in `/Users/coen/Developer/swift-institute/Research/` and `/Users/coen/Developer/swift-primitives/Research/`; this index carries pointers to those external canonical docs.

documents.topic: Notes on possible interaction between this package and stdlib SE-0519 (Borrow<T> / Inout<T>). Captures current state (mirroring shape on Swift 6.3.1 via _read / nonmutating _modify before SE-0507 stabilises) and several plausible scenarios for what may happen when SE-0519 stabilises: deprecate package types in favour of stdlib equivalents, retain divergent types where stdlib ships a meaningfully different shape, or continue current shape if SE-0519 stalls. Explicitly non-committal; replaces the README's earlier 'Migration to stdlib SE-0519' four-step deprecation-plan prose, which asserted forecast as consumer fact.
documents.statusDetail: Contributor-facing notes only; not part of the source-stability commitment. Created 2026-05-01 to relocate the README's prior speculative migration-plan prose, per cohort README evaluator-pass remediation (swift-institute/Research/cohort-readme-evaluator-pass.md).

documents.topic: Pre-0.1.0 per-type evaluation on merits + naming review + completeness analysis. Reframes the earlier usage-centric v1.0.0 draft (SUPERSEDED within this document's changelog) to evaluate each Ownership type by its unique position in the 5-axis lattice (lifetime × mutability × ownership-multiplicity × sync × copyability), not by adoption count. Package is positioned as total for ownership. Recommends naming actions: rename Transfer.Box → Transfer.Erased (Cluster A, high priority — diametric collision with Rust Box<T>); rename Slot.Store result → Slot.Outcome (Cluster E — SUPERSEDED 2026-04-24 by removal; see naming-slot-store-result-enum.md v2.0.0); optional Transfer direction rename (Cluster B: Cell/Storage → Outgoing/Incoming) and Unique → Box (Cluster C, requires Cluster A). Identifies two completeness gaps (inbound AnyObject-zero-alloc transfer, inbound type-erased transfer) visible under the direction-symmetry rename. @unchecked Sendable breakdown: Category A (Slot, _Box, Box.Pointer) and B (Unique, Retained) are sound; D (Shared) is a compiler-limitation workaround; only Category C (Mutable.Unchecked) is the class region-based isolation (SE-0518 ~Sendable) replaces — already DEFERRED in the audit.

Per-module naming research for Ownership.Transfer.Box. Concludes: RENAME to Transfer.Erased. Rust's std::boxed::Box<T> is exclusive heap ownership (our Ownership.Unique); calling our type-erased transfer slot Box is diametrically wrong. 'Erased' matches the contract, aligns with Apple's type-erasure vocabulary (AnyHashable, any, AnySequence) without stealing the Any* prefix, and does not collide with academic terms of art. Blast radius: zero external consumers.

Per-module naming research for Ownership.Slot.Store (the ~Copyable result enum). v1.0.0 recommended rename to Slot.Outcome. v2.0.0 (IMPLEMENTED 2026-04-24) supersedes: the enum is removed entirely — store(_) returns Value? directly, matching stdlib Dictionary.updateValue(_:forKey:) shape. Ecosystem sweep confirmed zero external consumers of the enum cases; every external slot.store(_) site uses `_ = slot.store(...)` and is source-compatible with the Value? return. Builds green across swift-ownership-primitives (84/33 tests), swift-async-primitives, and swift-pool-primitives.
documents.statusDetail: v2.0.0 — enum removed; store(_) returns Value?

documents.topic: Per-module naming research for Ownership.Unique. v1.0.0 recommended RENAME to Ownership.Box (conditional on Transfer.Box → Transfer.Erased landing first). SUPERSEDED 2026-04-24 by naming-box-ecosystem-survey.md + experiments unified-vs-two-type-box-design + nested-type-generic-escape. Final action (IMPLEMENTED 2026-04-24): the Ownership.Unique NAME stays (it is the Institute Nest.Name rendering of SE-0517 UniqueBox; renaming to Box is rejected by Apple's own ecosystem-collision reasoning). The API is tightened to SE-0517 parity: .take() (mutating) → .consume() (consuming); .duplicated() → .clone(); drop .hasValue / .leak() / description / debugDescription; add var value { _read _modify }; storage becomes non-optional. 85 tests / 35 suites pass.
documents.statusDetail: type rename superseded; API rewrite implemented

documents.topic: Ecosystem-wide literature study of 'Box' naming across swiftlang/swift (compiler + stdlib + runtime), Apple stewardship packages (swift-nio, swift-crypto, swift-collections, swift-package-manager, swift-certificates), Swift Evolution proposals (SE-0517 UniqueBox accepted, SE-0527 RigidArray/UniqueArray in review), and Swift Forums naming debates. Documents Apple's full Unique* / Rigid* family strategy + the new Containers stdlib module. Key findings: (1) Swift stdlib has ZERO public bare 'Box' types — all '*Box' public types are qualified (NIOLockedValueBox, SealedBox, ThreadSafeBox, SendableBox); (2) Apple explicitly reserves bare 'Box' for a future CoW variant per LSG acceptance note; (3) Apple's chosen API uses consume() not take(); (4) McCall's matrix: Array/UniqueArray, Dictionary/UniqueDictionary, Set/UniqueSet, Box/UniqueBox. Revises A3 recommendation: rename Ownership.Unique → Ownership.UniqueBox and .take() → .consume() for full SE-0517 API parity. Reinforces A1 (Transfer.Box → Transfer.Erased). Leaves A2 (direction rename) orthogonal to this axis.

Per-module naming research for Ownership.Transfer.Cell + Ownership.Transfer.Storage (+ Retained, Box). Recommends: pair RENAME to Outgoing / Incoming (with Retained → Outgoing.Retained and Box → Outgoing.Erased). The direction axis is first-class but the current names hide it. Rust (std::sync::mpsc, tokio::sync::mpsc) consistently uses Sender/Receiver; session-types literature (Honda-Vasconcelos-Kubo 1998, Gay-Vasconcelos 2010) uses direction-named primitives. Rename surfaces the two known completeness gaps (Incoming.Retained, Incoming.Erased) as nameable positions. Blast radius: ~10 call sites across swift-kernel + swift-executors.

Per-module naming research for Ownership.Shared + Ownership.Mutable + Ownership.Mutable.Unchecked. Recommends: DO NOT RENAME. Apple's 25-year NSArray/NSMutableArray pattern is the canonical precedent — the current scheme IS that pattern. Explicit-pair alternative (Shared.Immutable / Shared.Mutable / Shared.Mutable.Unchecked) is academically motivated (Clarke-Potter-Noble 1998; Boyapati-Liskov-Shrira 2003) but adds nesting with 32+ external call sites across swift-async / swift-pool / swift-cache / swift-markdown-html-render. The seasoned-Swift-engineer reachability is better under the current names (one step vs two).
documents.statusDetail: recommend: do not rename — Apple precedent (NSArray/NSMutableArray)

crossRefDocuments.topic: Canonical protocol shape for `Ownership.Borrow.`Protocol`` — associatedtype `Borrowed` defaults to the sibling generic struct over `Self`. Frozen DECISION landed in commit `b3eb11b`.
Ecosystem-wide implementation plan for the `Ownership.Borrow.`Protocol`` unification. IMPLEMENTED 2026-04-23 across 7 sub-repos; zero residual `.View` references on the cascade surface.
Generalises the `Ownership.Borrow.`Protocol`` associatedtype-defaulting shape to other self-projection namespaces. The Borrow application is the first empirical ecosystem instance.
Capability-lift pattern — the `associatedtype default = Namespace<Self>` shape that `Ownership.Borrow.`Protocol`` uses.
Academic references grounding the capability-lift pattern.
Feature-flag choices around `BorrowAndMutateAccessors` (SE-0507) — the future state that makes `Ownership.Borrow._read` / `Ownership.Inout._modify` coroutines obsolete.
Motivates `Ownership.Borrow<~Escapable>` and the raw-address init path used for ~Escapable peek.
~Copyable closure-capture interactions that affect Ownership-type usage in `@Sendable` closures.
Consolidated current state of ~Copyable patterns across the ecosystem (supersedes `noncopyable-ownership-transfer-patterns.md`).
Coroutine-yielding vs plain-return lifetime models — informs the V12 `Ownership.Inout.value` accessor split (`get` + `_modify` for Copyable `Value`; `_read` + `_modify` for ~Copyable).
Naming conventions for nested view / borrowed types that affect `Ownership.Borrow` / `Ownership.Inout` adoption sites.
